fundamentals.name: iShares Silver Trust
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: The Trust seeks to reflect such performance before payment of the Trust's expenses and liabilities. It is not actively managed. The Trust does not engage in any activities designed to obtain a profit from, or to ameliorate losses caused by, changes in the price of silver.

news.paragraph: SLV, the world's largest silver ETF, has seen significant gains over the past year, but recent analysis highlights potential drawbacks including its 0.50% fee and a 28% tax rate, which may diminish investor returns. While one article predicts a surge after May 15th, another suggests a competing ETF, SIVR, might be a better option. The ongoing discussion questions whether SLV remains the optimal way to invest in silver for 2026.

headlines.title: SLV Is Up 132% in a Year, But Its 0.50% Fee and 28% Tax Rate Tell a Different Story
Best Silver Stocks for 2026 and How to Invest
SIVR Beats SLV. Here Is Why.
Prediction: This Silver ETF Will Take Off After May 15
SLV: The Largest Silver ETF in the World -- but Is It Still the Best Way to Own Silver in 2026?
